LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF MARIA SERRANO RAUDA'S MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD. THE MOTION IS UNOPPOSED AND GRANTED. COUNSEL MUST PROVIDE A FILLED-IN ORDER ON THE JUDICIAL COUNCIL FORM. THE ORDER SHOULD NOTE THE NEXT HEARING WHICH APPEARS TO BE A NOVEMBER 14 DISCOVERY MOTION. COUNSEL SHOULD NOTE THAT THE ORDER PERMITTING WITHDRAWAL AS COUNSEL OF RECORD IS NOT EFFECTIVE UNTIL SERVICE OF THE ORDER ON THE CLIENT, AND COUNSEL SHOULD TAKE STEPS TO RESPOND TO AND APPEAR AT THE DISCOVERY HEARING, OR TO HAVE IT CONTINUED. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER USING THE MANDATORY JUDICIAL COUNSEL FORM (MC-053) JUDGE: CURTIS E. KARNOW; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (302/CEK)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Dec-18-2023 Vacated

Notice Of Motion To Set Aside Order Granting Defendant'S Motion To Compel Discovery Responses And Deeming Request For Admissions Admitted
Off Calendar Dec-12-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom):

MILTON KATZ, Esq., Law Offices of, (415) 441-5400, for Plaintiff.

William H. Coke, Esq., of SMITH, KOYAMA & COSTELLO, (408) 392-8644, for Defendant.

RE: PLAINTIFF MARIA SERRANO RAUDA's Motion To Set Aside Order Granting Defendant'S Motion To Compel Discovery Responses And Deeming Request For Admissions Admitted.

Ruling is discussed. Upon conclusion of discussion, the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Off calendar. Motion to be re-set before the trial judge given the 12/18/23 trial date.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories, That The Request For Admissions Are Deemed Admitted And Demand For Inspection
Off Calendar Nov-14-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
William Coke, Esq. 408-983-1675 Smith Koyama & Costello for Defendant.
No other Apperance.

"THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF."

ARGUED. Mr. Coke apprises the Court of status of case that he has informed the other side of his appearance to contest the tentative ruling and that Plaintiff's counsel is moving to withdraw. Mr. Coke requests the motions be granted. The Court shares his concerns. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT ALFONSO GOBALEZA's Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories, That The Request For Admissions Are Deemed Admitted And Demand For Inspection.

The Court orders Mr. Coke to prepare a proposed order granting the motion and instructing the Plaintiff to file responses within 5 calendar days of this order and include language that the Court finds good cause to extend discovery cut-off to 11/20/23.

Judge Pro Tem: Paul Renne, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not reported. =(302/JPT)

MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
Off Calendar Nov-03-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances
Milton Katz, Esq., Law Offices of Milton Katz, 415-441-5400 miltonkatz@yahoo.com Attorne for
Plaintiff, Maria Serrano Rauda (counsel seeking withdrawal) via Zoom Conference
Maria Serrano Rauda, Esq., 415-410-8452, Plaintiff and is assited by Court Certifed Interpeter Daniel Navarro (in court appearance)
William H. Coke, Esq., Carbone, Smith & Koyama (408) 392-8644 Attorney for Defendant Alfonso Santos Gobalezafor via Zoom Conference

Defense counsel indicates to the court that he is not contesting the Court's tentative ruling, but that he is seeking clarification as to whether or not the tentative ruling will affect the upcoming trial date. Court indicates that the issue of the trial date was not before him this morning.

Hearing is held. Discussed. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF MARIA SERRANO RAUDA's MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD

The motion is unopposed and granted. Counsel must provide a filled-in order on the Judicial Council form. The order should note the next hearing which appears to be a November 14 discovery motion. Counsel should note that the order permitting withdrawal as counsel of record is not effective until service of the order on the client, and counsel should take steps to respond to and appear at the discovery hearing, or to have it continued.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order using the Mandatory Judicial Counsel Form (MC-053)

Judge: Curtis E. Karnow; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/CEK)
